name: Justin Jefferson
headline: Elite usage intact, just waiting on a real quarterback
body:
The volume that built Jefferson's reputation never left. In 2025 he ran 96.3% of routes, commanded a 30.1% target share and a 0.72 WOPR on 141 looks, with 18 red-zone and 10 end-zone targets. The collapse was purely downstream: his EPA per play cratered to -0.091 with a 49.6% success rate, and just two touchdowns despite that target diet. Yards per reception sank to 12.48 and catch rate to 59.6%, the fingerprints of broken quarterback play rather than a fading receiver.

Compare 2024, when the same workload produced 1,533 yards, 10 scores, a 0.417 EPA mark and 214.5 fantasy points. The role is identical; only the trigger man changed. With Kyler Murray or J.J. McCarthy stepping in, the touchdown and efficiency regression points strongly back up. Drafting him around WR6 and inside the top ten is a bet on quarterback competence, and the alpha usage makes that bet an easy one.
